openmeter/taxcode/adapter.purpose: Ent-backed persistence layer (taxcode.Repository) for tax codes and per-namespace organization default tax codes. All DB access is transaction-aware and namespace-scoped; this is the only place that talks to entdb.Client for taxcode tables.

patterns.name: Repository constructor with validated Config
patterns.description: New(Config) validates Client + Logger via models.Validator and returns taxcode.Repository, never the concrete *adapter.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (taxcode.Repository, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &adapter{db: config.Client, logger: config.Logger}, nil }

patterns.name: Tx/WithTx/Self transaction trio
patterns.description: adapter implements Tx (HijackTx), WithTx (rebind via NewTxClientFromRawConfig), and Self so entutils.TransactingRepo can rebind to the ctx-carried tx.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *adapter { txClient := entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &adapter{db: txClient.Client(), logger: a.logger} }

patterns.name: Wrap every method body in TransactingRepo
patterns.description: Each repo method calls input.Validate() then entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func...) (or TransactingRepoWithNoValue for void) so it joins an existing tx.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, a *adapter) (taxcode.TaxCode, error) { ... })

patterns.name: Namespace + soft-delete filtering
patterns.description: Reads filter taxcodedb.Namespace(...) and (unless IncludeDeleted) taxcodedb.DeletedAtIsNil(); deletes are soft via SetDeletedAt(clock.Now()).

patterns.name: Map Ent entity -> domain via Map*FromEntity
patterns.description: Entities are converted with MapTaxCodeFromEntity / mapOrganizationDefaultTaxCodesFromEntity; never return *db.TaxCode upward. Expand edges loaded conditionally and read via *OrErr().

patterns.name: Translate Ent errors to domain errors
patterns.description: db.IsNotFound -> taxcode.New*NotFoundError; db.IsConstraintError -> models.NewGenericConflictError. Raw Ent errors are never leaked.

patterns.name: Upsert via OnConflict on namespace partial-unique
patterns.description: Org defaults upsert uses OnConflict(ConflictColumns(FieldNamespace), ConflictWhere(IsNull(FieldDeletedAt))).UpdateNewValues() then re-reads via Get to honor Expand.

key_file_guides.role: Config, New, and the Tx/WithTx/Self transaction plumbing
key_file_guides.watch_for: Return taxcode.Repository not *adapter; WithTx must rebuild db from the raw tx config, not reuse a.db.
GetTaxCodeByAppMapping uses a raw JSONB '@>' containment query and sorts system-managed first then by CreatedAt/ID — preserve that ordering; UpdateTaxCode clears AppMappings when input list is empty.

key_file_guides.role: Get/Upsert org default tax codes with edge expansion
key_file_guides.watch_for: Expansion edges loaded only when input.Expand.* is set; mapping uses InvoicingTaxCodeOrErr()/CreditGrantTaxCodeOrErr() and errors if the requested edge wasn't loaded.

key_file_guides.role: MapTaxCodeFromEntity: single source of TaxCode entity->domain conversion
key_file_guides.watch_for: AppMappings read via lo.FromPtr (nullable); Metadata wrapped with models.NewMetadata; nil entity returns an error.

anti_patterns: Returning *db.TaxCode or any Ent type to callers instead of mapping to taxcode domain types
Querying a.db directly without entutils.TransactingRepo, breaking transactional composition with the service layer
Omitting the Namespace or DeletedAtIsNil filter, leaking cross-namespace or soft-deleted rows
Leaking raw Ent errors instead of converting via db.IsNotFound / db.IsConstraintError to taxcode/models errors
Hard-deleting tax codes instead of soft-delete via SetDeletedAt(clock.Now())

decisions.decision: App-mapping lookup is a JSONB containment query (app_mappings @> [...]) plus in-Go stable sort
decisions.rationale: A single tax code can carry multiple app mappings and multiple codes can match; system-managed seeds must win deterministically over user duplicates.

decisions.decision: Org default tax codes use a partial-unique upsert keyed on namespace where deleted_at is null
decisions.rationale: Exactly one active default row per namespace, kept idempotent and stable (CreatedAt/ID unchanged) across repeated upserts.

code_examples.scenario: Adapter CRUD method: validate, transact, query namespace-scoped, translate errors, map

openmeter/taxcode/service.purpose: Business-logic layer (taxcode.Service) over taxcode.Repository: enforces cross-field invariants (namespace ownership, system-managed protection, org-default protection) and orchestrates the get-or-create-by-app-mapping flow inside transactions.

patterns.name: Service constructor with validated Config
patterns.description: New(Config) validates Adapter (taxcode.Repository) + Logger and returns *Service asserted against taxcode.Service.

patterns.name: Validate then transaction.Run delegating to adapter
patterns.description: Every method: input.Validate() then transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func...) (RunWithNoValue for void). Business checks live inside the tx callback before delegating.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) (taxcode.TaxCode, error) { return s.adapter.CreateTaxCode(ctx, input) })

patterns.name: Cross-field guards inside the transaction
patterns.description: UpsertOrganizationDefaultTaxCodes re-fetches both tax code IDs via GetTaxCode to prove namespace ownership before upserting; Update/Delete fetch existing first to check IsManagedBySystem and org-default usage.
patterns.example: if _, err := s.GetTaxCode(ctx, taxcode.GetTaxCodeInput{NamespacedID: models.NamespacedID{Namespace: input.Namespace, ID: input.InvoicingTaxCodeID}}); err != nil { return ..., err }

patterns.name: System-managed protection with AllowAnnotations bypass
patterns.description: Update/Delete reject IsManagedBySystem() codes with NewGenericConflictError(ErrTaxCodeManagedBySystem) unless input.AllowAnnotations is set (internal seeding path).

patterns.name: Concurrency-safe get-or-create
patterns.description: GetOrCreateByAppMapping looks up, creates on not-found, and on conflict re-reads; an orphaned key (conflict then still-not-found) returns ErrTaxCodeOrphanedKey rather than poisoning the pg tx.

key_file_guides.watch_for: Holds only adapter + logger; no direct DB client. Assert taxcode.Service interface compliance.
Delete blocks org-default and system-managed codes; GetOrCreate derives key as fmt.Sprintf("%s_%s", AppType, TaxCode) and must distinguish orphaned-key from generic not-found.

key_file_guides.role: Get/Upsert org defaults with namespace-ownership validation
key_file_guides.watch_for: Both InvoicingTaxCodeID and CreditGrantTaxCodeID are GetTaxCode-checked so a code from another namespace surfaces as IsTaxCodeNotFoundError, not a silent cross-tenant link.

key_file_guides.role: Service tests for system-managed, org-default protection, app-mapping preference
key_file_guides.watch_for: Asserts ValidationIssue.Code() equals taxcode.ErrCodeTaxCodeManagedBySystem / ErrCodeTaxCodeIsOrganizationDefault; system-managed seed must be preferred over user duplicate by GetTaxCodeByAppMapping.

key_file_guides.role: Service tests for upsert idempotency, cross-namespace rejection, expand
key_file_guides.watch_for: Idempotent upsert must keep row ID and CreatedAt stable; cross-namespace tax codes must not resolve.

anti_patterns: Calling adapter methods outside transaction.Run, losing atomicity across the validate-then-mutate guards
Allowing org-default upsert without re-verifying both tax code IDs belong to the namespace (cross-tenant leak)
Bypassing the IsManagedBySystem() check on Update/Delete without honoring AllowAnnotations
Putting Ent/DB queries directly in the service instead of going through taxcode.Repository
Treating a post-conflict not-found as a normal not-found instead of ErrTaxCodeOrphanedKey, risking a poisoned pg transaction

decisions.decision: Namespace ownership of referenced tax codes is enforced in the service, not the DB FK
decisions.rationale: Org defaults reference tax codes by ID only; the service re-fetches each by (namespace, id) so a foreign-namespace ID surfaces as not-found rather than a valid but cross-tenant link.

decisions.decision: Get-or-create handles the create-conflict race explicitly
decisions.rationale: Concurrent first-touch of the same app mapping must converge on one row; the retry read plus orphaned-key error keeps the surrounding Postgres transaction usable.

code_examples.scenario: Service method: validate, transact, business guard, delegate to adapter

openmeter/taxcode/testutils.purpose: Test harness for the taxcode domain: spins up a real Postgres-backed Ent adapter + service and provides fixture helpers (CreateTaxCode, SetupNamespaceDefaults) for taxcode service/adapter tests.

patterns.name: TestEnv built from concrete constructors
patterns.description: NewTestEnv wires testutils.InitPostgresDB -> taxcodeadapter.New -> taxcodeservice.New directly, not via app/common DI, to avoid test import cycles.
patterns.example: adapter, _ := taxcodeadapter.New(taxcodeadapter.Config{Client: client, Logger: logger}); svc, _ := taxcodeservice.New(taxcodeservice.Config{Adapter: adapter, Logger: logger})

patterns.name: Idempotent close via sync.Once
patterns.description: Close() runs once and shuts down ent client, EntDriver, and PGDriver; tests register t.Cleanup(env.Close).

patterns.name: Explicit schema migration step
patterns.description: Tests call env.DBSchemaMigrate(t) which runs Client().Schema.Create against the test DB before exercising the service.

patterns.name: Fixture helpers with generated defaults
patterns.description: CreateTaxCode fills Namespace/Key/Name from testutils.NameGenerator when empty and lets an optional CreateTaxCodeInput override fields; SetupNamespaceDefaults seeds two tax codes and upserts org defaults.
patterns.example: input.Namespace = namespace; if input.Key == "" { input.Key = generated.Key }

key_file_guides.role: TestEnv struct, NewTestEnv, DBSchemaMigrate, Close, CreateTaxCode, SetupNamespaceDefaults
key_file_guides.watch_for: NewTestEnv already registers a t.Cleanup(Close); the second t.Cleanup(env.Close) in tests is safe only because Close is sync.Once-guarded. Discard logger via testutils.NewDiscardLogger keeps test output clean.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common wiring to build the service, which creates test-only import cycles — build from taxcodeadapter.New / taxcodeservice.New instead
Sharing a single namespace across subtests that mutate org defaults; helpers use freshly generated namespaces per scenario
Forgetting env.DBSchemaMigrate(t) before service calls, so the taxcode tables don't exist

decisions.decision: Harness constructs adapter+service from underlying constructors rather than DI
decisions.rationale: Keeps taxcode test dependencies independent from app/common so unrelated wiring additions cannot introduce import cycles into this package.

code_examples.code:
env := taxcodetestutils.NewTestEnv(t)
t.Cleanup(func() { env.Close(t) })
env.DBSchemaMigrate(t)
ns := testutils.NameGenerator.Generate().Key
env.SetupNamespaceDefaults(t.Context(), t, ns)
